evidences.title: Summer Programming Camps – Exploring Project-Based Informal CS Education in a Rural Community | International Journal of Computer Science Education in Schools
evidences.content: camp curriculum may help students not only have fun with code but also learn fundamental programming and computational thinking skills through game development. Our annual week-long programming camp at a rural community utilized a project-based learning approach through game development in Python. Findings showed that students were able to achieve basic abstraction and algorithmic thinking but not code analysis and debugging skills. On their motivation to pursue computing careers, results did not show any difference before and after the camp due to their prior existing interest in attending the camp. [...] This paper makes several considerations for organizing short project-based programming camps to support programming knowledge and motivation. The paper addresses how current research has not explored how summer programming camps helped students not only increase interest to pursue computing career, but also increase their programming knowledge. Informal CS education through summer programming camps provides K-12 students the opportunity to learn how to code through fun and interactive activities outside of their typical classroom experiences. For rural communities with limited...

evidences.content: that it may not have been appropriate to test students to analyze and debug code during the assessment since we did not intentionally cover it during the camp. In the next programming camp, we plan to either simplify our programming camp assessment questions to include and cover simpler forms of code analysis and debugging and/or remove these questions. Thirdly, we recognize that the participants self-selected to participate in the programming camp and students already came in with some basic understanding. Maintaining interest after sparking initial interest increases the International Journal of Computer Science Education in Schools, September 2022, Vol. 5, No. 4 ISSN 2513-8359 11 likelihood of future pursuit of a related career (Christensen et al., 2014; Hidi & Renninger, 2006; Hirsch [...] A systematic review of empirical studies. evidences.title: Outreach – Computer Science
evidences.content: ### Computer Science Summer Camps The University of Alabama Department of Computer Science within the Lee J. Styslinger Jr. College of Engineering hosts an annual series of Computer Science camps. A week-long (commuter or residential dorm) camp is available for high school students to prepare for the College Board’s AP CS Principles course. Participants will be taught multiple topics of Computer Science while learning to program a computer in several exciting contexts while learning about the basics of the AP CSP course. Students in the high school camp have attended from over 20 different states, but our current focus is supported by grants that only enroll Alabama students. The Computer Science camps are taught in a manner that encourages self-exploration across multiple projects. evidences.content: Mr. Mickey Stemen ##### Mr. Mickey Stemen Mr. Mickey Stemen has been teaching computer science at Pensacola Christian College for over 30 years, specializing in networks, cryptography, programming languages, and computer architecture. During the summers, he serves as a programmer in the information technology department working on database, desktop, and web programming. He has even been involved in programming in the PCC Planetarium. Mr. Stemen loves to help students discover a passion for computer science, problem-solving, and creativity. evidences.title: How to Plan a Summer Science Camp | Get them to love learning!
evidences.content: Home Science Tools Resource Center Teaching Resources & Guides > How to Teach Science Tips > How to Plan a Summer Science Camp # How to Plan a Summer Science Camp ABOUT THE AUTHOR: After teaching a summer science camp for the past four years in a row, Debbie Schaner is a resident expert. Debbie helped her husband Frank start Home Science Tools back in 1994. Since that time, she has had four homeschooled children graduate from high school and go on to college. She is now a grandmother to six children, who she passes the joy of learning science to as often as possible. Science camps are one way Debbie does that. [...] ### Organize and Plan The best way to get this all accomplished in 4 hours a day is to have everything prepared ahead. I have gathered, sorted and cut out each item I need for each day. I also like to set aside the entire week so that each evening I can clean up and be ready for the next day. Then I cut up all the food for the following day’s lunch and make any preparations I need. ## Planning a Summer Science Camp: The Details ### Timeline 1. I begin the day with prayer and a song that we sing all week. I then introduce the subject in a fun way. For example, when st...

evidences.content: Registration Now Open! Note: Perfect for students excited about tech and computer science but new to coding. You’ll learn the basics together — no experience required! If the registration cost presents a barrier to participation, please get in touch for needs-based assistance: outreach@cs.wisc.edu. [...] University of Wisconsin–Madison Computer Sciences College of Computing & Artificial Intelligence # CS Explorations Summer Camp Dive into the world of computer science at UW–Madison! Spend an unforgettable week exploring cutting-edge research with university researchers and students. Each morning, step inside real labs to discover exciting fields like cryptography, robotics, and game design. In the afternoon, team up to build your own interactive project using Octostudio, a fun, block-based coding app. Who: Open to students entering 5th – 8th grades Where: Morgridge Hall (1205 University Ave) When: Mon-Thu, July 13-16, 9 AM-2:30 PM Cost: $350 (lunch included) Registration Now Open!

evidences.title: [PDF] STEM Summer Camps in the US: Knowledge and Context - Peer Asee
evidences.content: team selected a sample of articles for analysis, guided by predetermined factors, including: a) publishing dates between 1998 – 2017; b) publication in ASEE as it is one of the most prominent education avenues. From a pool of 729 identified articles, the research team randomly selected 24 articles relevant to 38 summer camps conducted within the US. In the second stage, the researchers developed a comprehensive list of twelve parameters, including "population, race, gender, age, targeted population, framework, theories used, context, learning outcome, delivery method, effect, and duration," which are integral components of any summer camp. Seven parameters were selected for analysis, as they could be associated with determining the "knowledge, context, and framework" of a summer camp. The [...] problems . Many summer camps implicitly implied the engineering design process approach. The engineering design process is a series of steps that engineers follow to develop solutions to problems. For instance, in Robotics camps campers were asked to build robots to perform specific tasks. To complete this task, campers would need to follow the steps of the engineering design process. Ide...

evidences.title: STEM Summer Camp Planning Guide
evidences.content: ## Be Strategic The long summer break from school provides a long stretch of time to plan activities for your child, and it may seem daunting. But with the right strategy, you can ensure they’re occupied and enjoying enriching activities that will help them get a jump on the new school year. Start with summer vacation plans: Whether you already have travel on your calendar or still need to book that trip, assess how much time before or after it you have, and then fill in those days. Be mindful of any family obligations or other events that you may need to plan around. ## Location, Location, Location [...] Stemful Stemful Stemful Stemful # STEM Summer Camp Planning Guide January 1, 2025 Now that the holidays are over and the New Year is here, it’s time to start planning for summer camp! Camps fill up quickly and there are some deals and promos to be found, so while summer may seem far off, now is the time to make a plan. But how do you go about choosing the right one? Read our tips on how to make next summer the best one ever for your child. evidences.content: Secure IT Summer Academy Students entering grades 6-8 Is computing taking over the world too quickly? Do you wonder if the technology you use is safe? Who created the code behind your app? Students interested in the growing field of cybersecurity learn at this camp to think like a cyber detective and employ design thinking to protect the things they interact with daily (smart technologies, wearables, internet sites). They use creative problem-solving to design, code and protect various systems designed to help people across many fields. TRAILS AI Summer Camp Students entering grades 10-11 [...] TRAILS AI Summer Camp Students entering grades 10-11 The TRAILS AI Summer Academy is a three-week, nonresidential computer programming and artificial intelligence summer camp for rising 10th and 11th grade students. Students learn how AI can be used to help people and an idea of what kinds of careers there are in AI. Please note, the first week of camp is completely asynchronous. Participants explore the field of AI through team projects, industry field trips and presentations from guest speakers. evidences.content: Instructors: Dr. Will Best & Dr. Sara Ramirez ### Game Design and Programming (GDP), June 8 - 12 add Camp Dates: June 8 - 12 Camp Purpose: The participants will learn how to use creativity and logic to create their own interactive video games. The camp combines creativity and basic programming to making their own version of a computer game. The Camp differs from other coding camps as it allows for the students to interact with computer science and programming concepts. The camp is focused on creating, developing, and coding games, not on just playing games or coding in general. Furthermore, the camp introduces concepts on how development and coding in general takes place on teams. [...] Any specific requirements for participants: HS students at the junior or senior level. No need for previous coursework on coding or programming experience but having it would help. Instructor: Dr. Esteban Parra Rodriguez ### Sports Analytics, July 6-10 add Camp Purpose: To expose students to sports statistics, analysis, and data science through statistical ideas and guided group projects through various sports. [...] Camp Description: The world is filled with too much data to analyze. Yet, the wo...

Camp Registration Portal No payment is due at the time of application. You will receive payment information after confirmation that you have received a spot at camp. evidences.content: ### 2026 Registration Dates & Details Online registration closes 21 days before the camp week start date (includes Daze/Fest). ### New Automated Waitlist [...] If an opening becomes available in a PRCS waitlisted program, the first individual on the waitlist will receive an email notification from (/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection) regarding their enrollment offer to register. You will need to log on to your PRCS Connect account to accept or reject enrollment. If the enrollment offer is not accepted or rejected within 36 hours of receiving the email, it will automatically be considered a rejection, and the individual will be removed from the waitlist, and the next eligible person on the waitlist will be contacted. For more information about the new automated waitlist system, visit our Automated Waitlist Frequently Asked Questions page.
